channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: Blue Origin rocket explodes on the launch pad during an engine-firing test
item.description: A rocket belonging to Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin exploded during a test at the launch pad Thursday night, ahead of a satellite launch planned for next week.
item.content:encoded:
A rocket belonging to Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin exploded during a test at the launch pad Thursday night, ahead of a satellite launch planned for next week.

(Image credit: John Raoux)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Shrey Parikh, 14, wins the Scripps Spelling Bee after a nail-biting 'spell-off'
item.description: This is the 101st year of the national spelling competition, and the third time it's been decided by a rapid-fire "spell-off" since the practice was introduced in 2021
item.content:encoded:
This is the 101st year of the national spelling competition, and the third time it's been decided by a rapid-fire "spell-off" since the practice was introduced in 2021

(Image credit: Allison Robbert)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Treasury Department prepares $250 bill with Trump's face on it
item.description: Congress needs to pass legislation in order for the bill's printing
item.content:encoded:
Congress needs to pass legislation in order for the bill's printing

(Image credit: KENT NISHIMURA)
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram

item.title: Former senior CIA officer took home gold bars and millions in cash, FBI says
item.description: David Rush, a former senior CIA officer, is accused of theft and making false statements. The trove of gold and cash went missing from his CIA storage space — but it was found in his Virginia home.
item.content:encoded: David Rush, a former senior CIA officer, is accused of theft and making false statements. The trove of gold and cash went missing from his CIA storage space — but it was found in his Virginia home.
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: 'Look to your elders': Alfre Woodard shares her secret to Hollywood longevity
item.description: Woodard says not giving up is the key to her long career. In the Netflix series The Boroughs, she plays a resident of a retirement community where something supernatural is preying on the residents.
item.content:encoded: Woodard says not giving up is the key to her long career. In the Netflix series The Boroughs, she plays a resident of a retirement community where something supernatural is preying on the residents.
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley

item.title: FIFA's World Cup ticket sales outraged fans. Now they are under investigation
item.description: FIFA has always had a peculiar way to sell tickets to the World Cup. It never faced any major issues — until prices soared for the 2026 tournament.
item.content:encoded:
FIFA has always had a peculiar way to sell tickets to the World Cup. It never faced any major issues — until prices soared for the 2026 tournament.

(Image credit: Win McNamee)
item.dc:creator: Rafael Nam

item.title: Orthodox Jewish women in Israel may now take a rabbinic exam, like men
item.description: Until now, only Orthodox Jewish men in Israel have been allowed to take exams to become rabbis. After a long campaign, women can now also take the test - but still can't become rabbis
item.content:encoded:
Until now, only Orthodox Jewish men in Israel have been allowed to take exams to become rabbis. After a long campaign, women can now also take the test - but still can't become rabbis

(Image credit: Ofir Berman for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: This is how close American households are to the financial edge
item.description: A new report shows that nearly half of U.S. households did not earn enough to cover their necessities in 2024.
item.content:encoded:
A new report shows that nearly half of U.S. households did not earn enough to cover their necessities in 2024.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: A federal judge in D.C. declines to block Trump's executive order on voting by mail
item.description: A federal judge in Washington, D.C., has declined to temporarily block President Trump's executive order that calls for restricting mail-in voting. Another judge may rule on the order soon.
item.content:encoded:
A federal judge in Washington, D.C., has declined to temporarily block President Trump's executive order that calls for restricting mail-in voting. Another judge may rule on the order soon.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang

item.title: U.S. street drug deaths keep dropping, but some Western states see deadly overdose surge
item.description: Researchers say some structural changes in treating addiction are helping, including wider access to overdose-reversing medication.
item.content:encoded:
Researchers say some structural changes in treating addiction are helping, including wider access to overdose-reversing medication.

(Image credit: John Raby)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann
